checks.name: A == B_inc^T B_inc: precision = Gram of sparse edge measurements (2 nonzeros per interior row, 1 per wall row)
X = B_inc A^{-1} == pinv(B_inc^T): responses are the pseudoinverse dual of the measurements
A^{-1} == X^T X: covariance = Gram of dense edge responses
every row e of X == solve(A, b_e), b_e = e-th measurement row (unit dipole injected across edge e; all 33 rows)
biorthogonality X^T B_inc == I_32 (dual bases)
Pi = X B_inc^T symmetric
Pi idempotent (Pi^2 == Pi): an orthogonal projection on edge space
spec(Pi) == {0 (x1), 1 (x32)}: rank n = 32, corank 1
GROUNDED resolution: Pi == I_33 - 11^T/33 exactly; null vector = the constant current (a uniform loop through ground)
diag(Pi) == 32/33 for every edge: the leverage score of each edge-datapoint; sum = 32 = rank (grounded Foster)
diag(Pi) == conductance x effective resistance in the ground-as-a-node CYCLE network (all 33 edges)
free-path contrast: B_full L_full^+ B_full^T == I_33 (tree => no cycles to project out); grounding removes exactly rank one
continuum closed form: X_{e,i} == h (x_i - 1[x_i >= x_e]) for all 33x32 entries (tail-head orientation; the guessed h(1_{x>s} - x) is its negative)
dipole row e == (tent at s = x_{e-1} minus tent at s = x_e)/h (adjacent columns of A^{-1}, walls padded 0)
unit-load assembly (Maxwell 1864): (A^{-1})_ij == sum_e r_e I_e^(i) I_e^(j), random conductances U(0.5,2), seed 42, all 32x32 entries
Maxwell-Betti reciprocity == symmetry of the response Gram
n=8: A Sigma == I entry-by-entry in Fraction arithmetic, zero tolerance
n=8: X = B_inc A^{-1} exact == closed form (i - 9*1[i>=e])/81, all 9x8 entries in Fractions
n=8: X^T B_inc == I_8 EXACTLY in Fractions (biorthogonality, zero tolerance)
n=8 worked entry: sum_e r_e I_e^(3) I_e^(6) == (A^{-1})_{3,6} == 1/81 exactly (currents -2/3,+1/3 and -1/3,+2/3; 9 edges)
P8: X_free = B L^+ == pinv(B^T)
P8: X_free^T X_free == L^+ (covariance Gram, free version)
P8: rows of X_free are gauge-fixed dipole fields: row e == L^+ b_e, each summing to 0 (perp to the constant gauge mode)
P8: Pi_free == I_7; diag = effective resistances = 1 per tree edge, Foster sum = 7 = vertices - 1
P8 honest note: X_free^T B == I - 11^T/8 (the CENTERING projector, not I: biorthogonality only modulo the gauge)
exact L2R predictor: phi_i == (n+1-i)/(n+2-i) == Sigma_{i,i-1}/Sigma_{i-1,i-1} and d_i^2 == h^2 phi_i (formula extends to i=1: d_1^2 = Sigma_11)
perfect whitener: (I-Phi)^T D^{-2} (I-Phi) == A and PCG converges in 1 iteration
const-D honesty: the scalar d_c^2 drops out of kappa/PCG/rho_opt (checked: spectrum shape invariant under D -> 7D at c = 0.8)

c=1 (random-walk prior) fails at the wall by RANK ONE: M_1 == A - e_n e_n^T/h^2 (the whitener never learns the right wall), spec(M^{-1}A) == {1 x 31, 33}, PCG shrugs it off in 2 iterations, plain Richardson DIVERGES (rho = 32)
stationary c-sweep computed: optimum c* = 0.915 (kappa = 10.83) const-D; c* = 0.895 (kappa = 9.68) matched-D; both beat Jacobi/c=0 (kappa = 440.7)
quantized-phi flavor: rounding the exact coefficients to b bits (matched variances); kappa falls 12.38 -> 1.024051 from 1 to 8 bits, monotone
B_reg = I - diag(A)^{-1}A == tridiag(1/2, 0, 1/2) (the half-step walk matrix; scalar D makes 11 SS2's two orderings equal), rho(B_reg) == cos(pi/33) = 0.995472
Neumann/walk series: partial sums D^{-1} sum B_reg^j -> A^{-1}, fitted rate e^{-0.004538} == rho(B_reg) = 0.995472 (long walks carry the far field)

truncated Neumann SPD for EVERY k tested including odd (min eig over k-grid = 2.079e-06 > 0): rho(B_reg) < 1 makes 1 - mu^(k+1) > 0 for all k -- the even-k-only safety rule is not needed on this chain
bandwidth(M_k^{-1}) == min(k, n-1): the walk length IS the range of the covariance model
clipped responses M_w^{-1} = X_w^T X_w SPD by construction and nonsingular at every w (min eig at w=0: 5.397e-05 > 0)
w = 31 (full window) recovers the exact colorer: kappa = 1.000000000000, PCG converges in 1 iteration
bandwidth(M_w^{-1}) == min(2w+1, n-1): window w => covariance range 2w+1
finite range cannot model the bridge: kappa(M_w^{-1}A) ~ w^(-1.98) over w in [1,16] (slope approx -2: halve the clipping, quadruple the conditioning debt)

arena.notation: B_inc = grounded incidence ((n+1) x n, +1/h tail, -1/h head, Vishnoi); B_reg = I - diag(A)^{-1}A (reports 11/15's B)

dual_basis_explorer.continuum_formula: X[e,i] = h * (x_i - 1[x_i >= x_e]),  x_e = e*h (tail-head orientation; negate for head-tail)
dual_basis_explorer.continuum_note: row e = (tent at x_{e-1} - tent at x_e)/h; equals h*x_i left of edge e and h*(x_i - 1) right of it

D_variants.const: d_c^2 = h^2 (1+c^2)/2 (matches interior diag of A; any scalar gives identical kappa/PCG/rho_opt)
D_variants.matched: D_ii = Sigma_ii - 2c Sigma_{i,i-1} + c^2 Sigma_{i-1,i-1} (true residual variance of the imperfect coefficient)

c1_story.identity: M_1 = A - e_n e_n^T / h^2 (const-D, d^2 = h^2)
c1_story.moral: rank-one wall defect: fatal for plain Richardson, two iterations for PCG

cost.model: two unit-diagonal bidiagonal solves at 2*nnz(W)=126 flops each + diagonal scale 2n; span = two sequential length-n substitution chains = 2(n-1)+1 = 63

ma_sliders.window_def: keep X[e,i] for e-1-w <= i <= e+w (w nodes beyond each endpoint of edge e); w=0 keeps only the edge's endpoints

kappa_vs_w_fit.law: kappa ~ w^(-1.98): finite-range covariance cannot capture the bridge (report 14's separator, from the covariance side)

cost_models.convention: report 11 SS5.2 / 14 SS4 PASS 30: 1 MAC = 2 flops, matvec = 2 nnz, vector op = 2N; PCG per iteration = 12N + one A-matvec (2*94) + one M-apply; setup 4N + one M-apply; total = setup + iters * per-iter
cost_models.neumann: apply = 2n (D^{-1}r) + k*(2*nnz(B_reg) + 2n) = 64 + 188k; span = 3k+1 rounds (each walk step embarrassingly parallel)
cost_models.clipped: apply = 2 * 2*nnz(X_w) (two sparse matvecs, X_w then X_w^T); span = 2*(1 + ceil(log2(2w+2))) -- constant-depth, embarrassingly parallel
cost_models.ar: apply = 316 flops but span = 63: the update_views lesson -- triangular solves are flop-cheap and depth-expensive
